task_completion.description: The initial baseline view of the DICOM file is displayed in a viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A DICOM viewer application is open, displaying an X-Ray Angiography image in its default, unmodified state.

task_completion.description: The initial baseline view is exported to state_1_initial.jpg.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save/export dialog or file manager shows the creation of 'state_1_initial.jpg' in the '~/DICOM/exports' directory.

task_completion.description: The image is modified with a 90-degree rotation, inverted grayscale mapping, and >300% zoom.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The DICOM viewer shows the image rotated by 90 degrees, the colors inverted (e.g., darks become light and vice versa), and heavily zoomed in so only a small portion of the image is visible.

task_completion.description: The modified view is exported to state_2_altered.jpg.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save/export dialog or file manager shows the creation of 'state_2_altered.jpg' in the '~/DICOM/exports' directory.

task_completion.description: The baseline view is restored by resetting all transformations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The DICOM viewer shows the image returned to its original state: unrotated, original grayscale mapping, and zoomed out to fit the screen.

task_completion.description: The restored baseline view is exported to state_3_restored.jpg.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save/export dialog or file manager shows the creation of 'state_3_restored.jpg' in the '~/DICOM/exports' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the DICOM viewer GUI to apply transformations and export images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the DICOM viewer's menus, buttons, or tools for rotating, inverting, zooming, and exporting, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) doing the work.

integrity.description: Transformations were applied within the DICOM viewer, not an external image editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rotated, inverted, and zoomed image is visible inside the main canvas of the DICOM viewer application itself.

integrity.description: The exported files were generated from the application, not spoofed or downloaded.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file creation process aligns with the export actions in the GUI, without evidence of downloading pre-made images from the web or copying them from hidden directories.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target export directory is /home/ga/DICOM/exports. The expected files are state_1_initial.jpg, state_2_altered.jpg, and state_3_restored.jpg. The loaded DICOM file is an X-Ray Angiography sample (0002.DCM).
